_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: GA federal House batch-7: hank-johnson (D GA-04), nikema-williams (D GA-05), lucy-mcbath (D GA-06, governor bid suspended 2025-03-31 — seeking House re-election; Laken Riley YES flip flagged), rich-mccormick (R GA-07), austin-scott (R GA-08, certified 2020 electors — deviation noted). All TRUE/FALSE cells cite roll calls, sponsored bills, ratings, or the member's own statements; nulls where no evidence. Party-line marquee votes with named crossovers used as HARD evidence per protocol.

profile.religion: Buddhist (Nichiren/Soka Gakkai; one of the first two Buddhists in Congress; Congressional Freethought Caucus member)
profile.background: Attorney; DeKalb County commissioner 2001-06; U.S. House since 2007. Heritage Action 0% current session / 6% lifetime; HRC 100% (118th); NumbersUSA 0% (F-).
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from clerk.house.gov roll calls, his own press releases, Heritage Action, HRC, NumbersUSA. Running for re-election 2026 (faces Postell and Truman in the 2026-05-19 D primary per Ballotpedia; D+27 district).

hank-johnson.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-10. Down-the-line progressive record: Heritage Action 0%/6% lifetime, HRC 100%, NumbersUSA 0% F-. Yea WHPA (Roll 2022-360), Nay Born-Alive twice (2023-29, 2025-27), Yea RFMA + Equality Act, Nay women's-sports bills twice, Nay Parents Bill of Rights, No on CBDC ban, Yea HR1/HR4, Nay SAVE Act, Nay HR2 + Laken Riley twice, Yea 2022 Assault Weapons Ban + Warren-Johnson gun-licensing bill. Closest cross-party overlap: Yea on 2002 Iraq AUMF repeal (Roll 2021-172, overlaps anti-interventionist right) — credited. Nay on Israel supplemental + 'present' on Iron Dome were left-flank objections, not conservative deviations; suggests no AIPAC alignment but funding data unverified (OpenSecrets blocked).

profile.background: Former VP of Public Policy, Planned Parenthood Southeast; GA state senator 2017-21; U.S. House since 2021 (John Lewis's seat); Congressional Progressive Caucus; House Financial Services. Stepped down as GA Democratic Party chair March 2025.

nikema-williams.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-10. Uniformly progressive record, no conservative cross-party deviations found. Former Planned Parenthood Southeast VP; introduced resolution declaring abortion 'a human right' (Dec 2025); co-leads Abortion Justice Act; Yea WHPA, Nay Born-Alive, cosponsored statutory-IVF-right bill; Yea RFMA + Equality Act, Nay women's-sports bill; Nay Parents Bill of Rights; No on CBDC ban; Yea HR1/HR4 (original cosponsor H.R.14), No SAVE Act; Nay Laken Riley + HR2 + HR30; Yea 2022 AWB, Giffords-endorsed, SAFES Act sponsor. Voted Yea on the Apr 2024 Israel supplemental (split from ~37 progressives — leadership-line, not a conservative deviation) while opposing the GOP Israel-only bill and the arms-shipment mandate. EMILY's List endorsee.

profile.religion: Christian (longtime member, Eagles Nest Church, Roswell GA; author of faith memoir 'Standing Our Ground: The Triumph of Faith Over Gun Violence')
profile.background: 30-year Delta flight attendant; became national spokesperson and Faith & Outreach Leader for Everytown for Gun Safety / Moms Demand Action after her son Jordan Davis was murdered in 2012; U.S. House since 2019 (GA-6 2019-23, GA-7 2023-25, GA-6 since 2025). Heritage Action 0%.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized. CURRENCY: explored a 2026 governor run (filed 2025-03-05) but SUSPENDED it 2025-03-31 citing her husband's cancer-surgery complications; ran unopposed in the 2026-05-19 D primary for GA-6 and faces R Kevin Martin in November (GPB, The Hill, AJC, Ballotpedia).

lucy-mcbath.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-10. Progressive record (Heritage 0%, Planned Parenthood 100%, NumbersUSA ~1%, EMILY's List ~$200K career receipts) with ONE significant cross-party deviation: voted YES on the Laken Riley Act (1/7/2025, one of 48 Democrats), FLIPPING from her March 2024 No — documented in cell notes (mandatory detention, not deportation, so the deportation cell stays null). Signature issue is gun control: lead sponsor of the federal red-flag bill (FERPO, passed House 2022, reintroduced 2026), H.R.8 and AWB-2022 cosponsor, Everytown/Giffords-backed. Openly Christian with a published faith memoir — credited on public profession of faith. Pro-Israel PAC receipts $271,663 (WRMEA/FEC, Apr 2024). Governor bid suspended 2025-03-31; seeking GA-6 re-election 2026.

profile.religion: Christian (member, Gwinnett Church — North Point network; 20+ years youth ministry, high-school small-group leader)
profile.background: Emergency-room physician (MD, Morehouse School of Medicine); 20+ years USMC CH-53E pilot and Navy Medical Corps, deployed Afghanistan/Persian Gulf; won the new GA-6 in 2022, moved to the redrawn GA-7 after the 2023 court-ordered redistricting. Heritage Action 91% (119th), 85% (118th), 88% lifetime.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized. Seeking GA-7 re-election (on the 2026-05-19 R primary ballot per Ballotpedia); declined to run for the 2026 Senate seat. Not to be confused with Sen. Dave McCormick (R-PA).

rich-mccormick.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-10. Strong conservative record (Heritage 88% lifetime): Life at Conception Act cosponsor, SBA Candidate Fund-endorsed; lead sponsor PROTECTS Act (bans federal funding of minor gender-transition procedures); authored Parents Bill of Rights amendments + Yea; Anti-CBDC Act cosponsor; deficit hawk — No on the Fiscal Responsibility Act debt deal, Nov 2023 CR, and Mar 2024 omnibus ('spending money we don't have'); SAVE Act Yea + cosponsor; Yea HR2 + Laken Riley. DEVIATION (vs. anti-aid wing): Yea on the Apr 2024 $61B Ukraine supplemental ('I support standing with Ukraine'); led the Tlaib censure. Heritage misses in the 118th (85%) unidentified. RFMA predates his term (seated Jan 2023) — no vote exists. Gun cells null pending NRA-PVF/VoteSmart pull; Track AIPAC lists him but amount unverified.

profile.background: Insurance businessman; GA legislature 1997-2010; U.S. House since 2011; House Armed Services + Agriculture (chairs the General Farm Commodities, Risk Management, and Credit subcommittee); ran against Jim Jordan for Speaker Oct 2023 (lost 124-81). Heritage Action 92% (119th), 62% (118th), 78% lifetime.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from direct govtrack/clerk roll calls, congress.gov, his own releases. Running for a 9th term in 2026 with no primary challenger (AJC); Democrats Justin Lucas and Kelly Esti in the general.

austin-scott.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-10. Establishment-conservative/defense-hawk profile: social-issue record uniformly conservative (Life at Conception Act cosponsor, Born-Alive Yea, Nay RFMA + Equality Act, Yea women's-sports bills twice, Yea Parents Bill of Rights, Yea SAVE Act twice, Yea HR2 + Laken Riley, NRA-PVF endorsed 2018/2020, Yea CBDC ban + all three anti-CBDC amendments, Yea terminating the COVID national emergency + pressed DOD on the Guard/Reserve mandate). DEVIATIONS flagged: (a) voted to certify 2020 electors — Nay on both AZ and PA objections, Jan 6-7 2021 (his framing: 'Votes to Uphold Constitution'); (b) Yea on the Apr 2024 $61B Ukraine supplemental (though also Yea on the Cammack amendment to strip non-military funds); (c) FISA 702: No on the Biggs warrant requirement in the 212-212 tie + Yea on RISAA reauth; (d) Nay on repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF — scored FALSE on the restraint cell; (e) mixed spending record — Yea FRA debt deal + Sept 2023 clean CR, but Nay on the Mar 2024 $1.2T omnibus (deficit cell held null as mixed); Heritage 62% in the 118th.
